title: ANGELShmm
wikitext:
[]: ANGELS...hmm???             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sat Dec 04, 2010 6:49 am well im surely they drew out blood after they got beaten so im sure they are organic... but, why they fight in their own ways?? i mean at least they know that their objective is being guarded by evangelions...so at least they should have a teamwork or even just a simple tandem in order to be successful... and one more thing... some angels are seen came from the atmosphere, from the sea.. but why is there a angel sent in to earth in embryonic form?? (Sandalphon was discovered in an embryonic state deep inside the Mount Asama volcano.) and im sure angels have 1 objective only... but why this angel named Gaghiel stays in water and attacks the UN fleet?? does he know that there is something important there? the Adam embryo i mean

[]:             Quote Post by Staffy3 » Sat Dec 04, 2010 7:50 am I think that the Angels can detect Adam/Lilith as their attacks are specifically aimed a the two. I would hazard a guess at their reasoning for attacking singularly, one at a time is merely psychological, to cause cockiness and worry in the Eva pilots, as is seen in later episodes. That and the fact that a three episode series wouldn't be memorable. Sometimes a show must defy logic for entertainment. :)

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Sat Dec 04, 2010 10:16 am I imagine they don't work together because they don't have knowledge of one another. Also no one angel is the same as another and each have their own idea of what they want third impact to be. Even humans can't agree on what they want from it. Seele has one idea of instrumentality, Gendo has another and Shinji has the one we see. As for their different forms, they were all born from Adam and spread throughout the Earth. Each one I imagine from some form of embryonic state, though Sandalphon was the only one we get to see before maturity.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Sat Dec 04, 2010 12:32 pm skikes wrote: Each one I imagine from some form of embryonic state, though Sandalphon was the only one we get to see before maturity.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sat Dec 04, 2010 1:09 pm During Second Impact, Adam's eggs were spread all over the place: some went into the ocean, some into space, and one even landed in a volcano. At this point, the eggs were completely identical. As they slowly hatched, one by one, they formed their own image, and then began to follow their individual objectives. It can be hypothesized that each Angel's attack happened shortly after they hatched, with enough time in between for them to travel from their place of birth to Nerv— this is no including Tabris, of course. When Nerv discovered Sandalphan, it had not hatched yet. The reason they never attack Nerv in groups is likely because, as skikes said, the Angels aren't aware of each others existence. And even if they were aware of each other, what need would they have to work with one another? Aside from the Evas, they Angels have no threat and they probably don't believe they're in any danger as they're following their own respective objectives.

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Sat Dec 04, 2010 7:17 pm +1 for the theory that Second Impact scattered a heap of angel eggs over the planet. But I think it is also possible that "where do angels come from" is something that the Eva creators never bothered with (eg "they just appear and attack us. stop asking irrelevant questions") Interesting theory that the angels can not perceive the presence of other angels, so they do not work together. I think it is also possible that they have a shared memory, they appear to get smarter throughout the series as each angel learns from the previous angel's mistakes.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Sun Dec 05, 2010 1:27 am nomis1242 wrote: Interesting theory that the angels can not perceive the presence of other angels, so they do not work together. I think it is also possible that they have a shared memory, they appear to get smarter throughout the series as each angel learns from the previous angel's mistakes.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Sun Dec 05, 2010 4:01 pm Assasinate wrote: shared memory?? hmm well quite posible but.. if they are REALLY been memory shared,why they dramatically changed too much as to Infector (Bardiel) to Brute force (Zeruel)??

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Mon Dec 06, 2010 5:42 am AshPhoenix wrote: I think that's just more of the Angels trying out different tactics to see which works best.

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Dec 06, 2010 9:32 pm nomis1242 wrote: ... but if pressed I'm sure I could make up some convoluted reasoning...that's half the fun of eva)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 07, 2010 12:00 am nomis1242 wrote: I realise the transition from Leliel to Bardiel is a gaping hole in my theory.

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Tue Dec 07, 2010 12:44 am Reichu wrote: It's very heavily implied that Bardiel controlled Eva-03 through the pilot.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 07, 2010 12:58 am You don't have to be mocking. There's discussion I can link you to that goes into more detail if you're genuinely interested.

[]:             Quote Post by nomis1242 » Tue Dec 07, 2010 2:22 am Reichu wrote: You don't have to be mocking. There's discussion I can link you to that goes into more detail if you're genuinely interested.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Tue Dec 07, 2010 7:07 am well so i see... the shared memory stuff is not enough... well.. apparently the brute force tactic is already applied to Sachiel, but if your giong to compare to Zeruel well that's quite too far enough... and also... are these angel names are just a numerical term for every enemy?? or its just already their names??

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Dec 07, 2010 4:05 pm Assasinate wrote: and also... are these angel names are just a numerical term for every enemy?? or its just already their names??

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Dec 07, 2010 4:20 pm They are the names given to the Angels by humanity, likely Seele in accordance to the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Assasinate » Tue Dec 07, 2010 9:29 pm i see... well if they already given these names to them?? so they already know about them in a long time before they attacked us right??

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Dec 07, 2010 9:54 pm ^ Well, Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls had a scenario, but I doubt the Angel's names were given by the FAR, or even by Adam...as Sachi said, humans gave the Angels those names. So to answer the question, it's implied that some people (like Seele and Gendo) knew that the Angels would attack again, just not how.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 08, 2010 12:46 pm AshPhoenix wrote: So to answer the question, it's implied that some people (like Seele and Gendo) knew that the Angels would attack again, just not how.
